name: dotenv listed as dev dependency in docs but missing from package.json
description: CLAUDE.md references dotenv as a dev-only dependency for local development. It is not in package.json devDependencies. The 'start:dev' script uses --env-file (Node.js native flag, v20.6+) so dotenv may not actually be needed. Clarify: if --env-file covers local dev, remove the docs reference; if dotenv is needed for some path, add it to devDependencies.

name: CRITICAL: image content blocks are MCP-schema invalid
description:
The server returns { type: 'image', url: '...' } content blocks. The official MCP SDK does NOT accept this shape for tool results. Valid content block types are: text, image (with data + mimeType for base64 binary), resource, resource_link.

Consequence: tools/list succeeds, but tools/call fails with 'MCP error -32602: Invalid tools/call result' when using the official SDK Client + StdioClientTransport. The server appears fine in unit tests and may work in tolerant clients (e.g., Claude Desktop has lenient parsing) but is not interoperable with spec-compliant MCP clients.

Evidence locations:
  - src/tools/photo-search.ts: returns { type: 'image', url: photo.src.medium }
  - src/tools/video-search.ts: returns { type: 'image', url: bestFile?.link || video.image }
  - src/tools/get-details.ts: same pattern for both photo and video
  - src/index.ts: server instructions explicitly describe this invalid shape as intended

Fix options (pick one per result kind):
  A. resource_link (recommended for remote URLs) — { type: 'resource_link', uri: url, mimeType: 'image/jpeg', name: 'Preview' }
  B. Remove the image block entirely and rely on the markdown ![]() link in the text block as the visual fallback

Do NOT fetch and base64-encode remote images — that would add ~5 fetches and ~1MB bloat per response.

After fixing:
  - Remove the 'as any' cast in registerTool calls (they exist because the return type was wrong)
  - Update src/index.ts server instructions to describe valid content shapes only
  - Add MCP integration test that validates tools/call succeeds with the official SDK

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #1 (Critical). Related: mrtdippu (as any casts), y1adujax (docs encode the bug).
result: Replaced { type: 'image', url } with { type: 'resource_link', uri, name, mimeType } in all three tools. Removed local ContentBlock type — now re-exported from SDK. Typed handler args (z.infer<schema>) eliminated all 'as any' casts. Added tests/tools/schema-compliance.test.ts validating all formatters against SDK CallToolResultSchema and ContentBlockSchema. 51/51 tests pass, tsc clean.

name: Attribution URLs derived from names instead of API fields
description:
The server constructs Pexels profile URLs by lowercasing the display name and stripping non-alphanumeric characters. The Pexels API already returns the canonical profile URL in every response. The derived URLs are frequently wrong.

Broken examples from live API (verified by Pi evaluation):
  Name: Griffin Wooldridge  → derived: /\@griffinwooldridge  actual: /\@griffinw
  Name: Aaron Ulsh          → derived: /\@aaronulsh          actual: /\@aulsh99
  Name: Şeyhmus Kino        → derived: /\@eyhmuskino         actual: /\@seyhmuskino
  Name: Çağrı KANMAZ        → derived: /\@arkanmaz           actual: /\@cagrikanmaz

The derivation breaks for users with: custom handles, numeric suffixes, accented/non-ASCII characters, underscores/hyphens, or shortened handles.

API fields to use instead:
  - Photos: photo.photographer_url (already on PexelsPhoto type)
  - Videos: video.user.url (already on PexelsVideo type)

Affected locations:
  - src/tools/photo-search.ts: formatPhotoResult() — derives photographerHandle from photo.photographer
  - src/tools/video-search.ts: formatVideoResult() — derives photographerHandle from video.user.name
  - src/tools/get-details.ts: inline in both photo and video branches

Fix:
  - In formatPhotoResult: use photo.photographer_url directly
  - In formatVideoResult: use video.user.url directly
  - In get-details.ts: same for both branches
  - Delete the handle-derivation logic entirely (toLowerCase/replace chain)
  - Add tests with accented names and custom-handle fixtures to confirm correct URLs

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #3 (High).

name: force_refresh skips writing back to cache
description:
When force_refresh is true, the cache read is bypassed (correct), but the freshly fetched result is also NOT written back to cache. This means:
  - The single force_refresh call gets fresh data (good)
  - The next normal request still receives the old stale cached value until TTL expires (bad)

Caller expectation: force_refresh means 'bypass stale cache AND repopulate it with fresh data'.
Actual behavior: force_refresh means 'bypass cache entirely for this one call'.

Fix:
  - Always write the fetched result to cache after a successful API call, regardless of force_refresh
  - force_refresh should only control whether the cache is READ, not whether it is WRITTEN
  - This applies to all three tools: photo-search, video-search, get-details
  - Add a regression test: call with force_refresh=true, then call again without it and assert the fresh data is returned (not stale)
  - Document the semantics clearly in README

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #5 (Medium).

name: Add structured output (outputSchema + structuredContent)
description:
Tool responses are markdown text blobs. Downstream consumers (UI builders, agents, automation scripts) must regex-parse formatted text to extract IDs, URLs, and dimensions. This makes integration brittle and card-rendering dependent on alternating block order.

MCP supports outputSchema (declares the structured shape) and structuredContent (the typed payload). These should be added alongside the human-readable text block, not replacing it.

Target structuredContent shape for photos:
  {
    results: [{
      id: number,
      kind: 'photo',
      creatorName: string,
      creatorUrl: string,
      pageUrl: string,
      previewUrl: string,
      mediaUrl: string,
      mediaMimeType: 'image/jpeg',
      dimensions: { width: number, height: number },
      avgColor: string,
      alt: string
    }]
  }

Target shape for videos (add to above):
  {
    kind: 'video',
    durationSeconds: number,
    previewUrl: string,       // thumbnail image URL
    mediaUrl: string,         // best mp4 link
    mediaMimeType: 'video/mp4'
  }

Implementation:
  - Define the output schema as a Zod object and pass it to registerTool as outputSchema
  - Assemble structuredContent from typed API response data (not from parsing the text)
  - Return structuredContent alongside content[] in the CallToolResult
  - Keep the markdown text block as human-friendly fallback
  - This makes previewUrl vs mediaUrl vs pageUrl unambiguous — no more semantic mislabeling of mp4 links as image blocks

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #4 (High) and finding #8 (video semantically mislabeled as image).

name: Cache key computed twice per search call
description: In handlePhotoSearch and handleVideoSearch, makeCacheKey(params) is called once before the fetch (to check) and again inside the try block (to write). The key is pure and deterministic so the second call is redundant. Fix: compute once, store in a variable, reuse in both branches.

name: Video selector: document or specify tie-break behavior
description:
chooseBestVideo() in src/shared/video-selector.ts is not crashy and has a reasonable fallback path, but when two candidate files are equally close to 1920px wide, the tie is broken by whichever candidate appears first in the array (reduce returns the existing 'best' on equality). This is deterministic but undocumented.

Current behavior:
  - Empty list → undefined
  - No mp4 → first file (any format)
  - mp4 available → prefer 'hd' quality, then pick closest width to 1920
  - Tie on distance-to-1920 → first in array wins (implicit)

Issues:
  - 'hd' quality label is not the same as actual resolution — a 720p file labeled 'hd' would win over an unlabeled 1080p file
  - On a tie, 'first in array' may not be the most useful choice (e.g., prefer the larger file? the one with higher height?)
  - The fallback to non-mp4 first file is safe but undocumented

Fix options (pick one):
  A. Document current behavior with a comment in the function explaining the tie-break and fallback rules (minimal change)
  B. Add explicit tie-break: on equal distance, prefer larger width (i.e., go higher rather than lower)
  C. Consider filtering by resolution in addition to quality label: prefer files >= 1280px wide before applying hd preference

Add tests covering tie cases: two equal-distance candidates, two files with same width, hd vs non-hd at same distance.

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #11 (Low).

name: 'as any' casts in all three tool registrations
description: Each tool file casts its registerTool handler with 'as any' (e.g. async (args: unknown) => handlePhotoSearch(args) as any). The handler return type doesn't satisfy what the MCP SDK expects. Fix: align the return type of handle* functions with the SDK's CallToolResult type so the cast can be removed. May require adjusting the ContentBlock type or the return signature.
result: Resolved as side-effect of 14uufydg. Handlers now take z.infer<typeof schema> args and return Promise<CallToolResult> using SDK types. registerTool callbacks are plain arrow functions with no casts needed.

name: Repo hygiene: LICENSE file, maturity claim, SDK example
description:
Three related low-priority but user-facing quality issues:

1. Missing LICENSE file
   - README ends with a License section, but no LICENSE file exists in the repo
   - Without a license, the repo is technically all-rights-reserved by default
   - Fix: add a LICENSE file (MIT is appropriate for an unofficial community project)

2. 'Production-grade' overstatement
   - README describes the project as 'a production-grade MCP server'
   - Current state: critical protocol compliance failure, wrong attribution URLs, missing integration tests
   - Fix: tone this down until after the critical issues are resolved; 'A well-structured MCP server for Pexels' is accurate

3. No official SDK example client
   - Quick Start only shows Claude Desktop config JSON
   - There is no example showing tools/list + tools/call working via the official SDK
   - Fix: add a minimal Node.js example (e.g., examples/client.ts or a test) that:
       a) spawns the server via StdioClientTransport
       b) lists available tools
       c) calls pexels_search_photos and prints the result
   - This doubles as a smoke test and a documentation artifact
   - Also note: plain git clone fails in some environments (auth friction); gh repo clone works — add a note to README setup instructions

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #10 (Low) and finding #12 (environment/setup).

name: mcp-server-pexels: debug & refactor findings
description: Tracking issues identified during codebase review (2026-05-02). May overlap with separate findings. Tackle individually as subtasks.

name: Add structured debug logging / observability
description:
The server emits one startup log and nothing else. In agent environments — where tools are called automatically, repeatedly, and quickly — this makes troubleshooting very hard. Rate limiting is easy to hit silently. Cache effects can hide what's actually happening. API errors disappear without a trace.

What is currently missing:
  - No cache hit/miss logs
  - No query timing
  - No API error classification in logs (you only see the result the agent receives, not the underlying HTTP status)
  - No request correlation (can't connect a tool call to its API request in logs)
  - No result count (did we get 0 results because the query returned nothing, or because the API failed?)

Recommended approach:
  - Add a debug logging utility that writes to stderr (stdio MCP servers must never write to stdout)
  - Gate verbose logging behind a DEBUG=1 or LOG_LEVEL=debug env var (default: off)
  - Log per tool call: tool name, normalized params, cache hit/miss, API round-trip time (ms), result count, any error status
  - Log on API errors: HTTP status, endpoint, whether retry is possible and when
  - Keep startup log; optionally add a 'ready' log after server.connect() resolves

Implementation note: all logging MUST use console.error() — stdout is reserved for JSON-RPC.

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #9 (Medium). README already acknowledges this as a future improvement.

name: Missing test coverage for tool handlers and video/get-details tools
description:
The test suite passes completely while the server's core user journey — 'can an MCP client call this tool successfully?' — fails. This gives a false sense of confidence.

Missing coverage by layer:
  1. Unit: handlePhotoSearch, handleVideoSearch, handleGetDetails not tested (cache hit, cache miss, force_refresh, isError paths)
     video-search.ts and get-details.ts have zero test files at all
  2. Tool-layer: no tests for API error → tool error mapping (401, 429, 5xx)
  3. MCP integration: no test that spawns the built server via StdioClientTransport and calls tools using the official SDK Client — this is the layer that caught the critical protocol compliance failure in the Pi evaluation

Required additions:
  - tests/tools/video-search.test.ts (formatVideoResult, handleVideoSearch)
  - tests/tools/get-details.test.ts (handleGetDetails, photo and video paths)
  - Expand tests/tools/photo-search.test.ts with handlePhotoSearch integration (cache hit, miss, force_refresh, error paths)
  - tests/integration/mcp-client.test.ts — real stdio server spawn, Client.callTool(), assert result validates against MCP schema
  - Error path tests for 401, 429 (with/without reset header), 500, 404 in api-client and tool handlers

The MCP integration test layer is non-negotiable for any published MCP server.

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #7 (Medium) and finding #1 (Critical).

name: Rate-limit errors silently swallowed
description:
api-client.ts throws a generic Error on any non-2xx HTTP response. Tool catch blocks always call formatPexelsError('not_found') regardless of what actually failed. This collapses four distinct failure modes into one misleading message:
  - 401 Unauthorized → should say: check PEXELS_API_KEY
  - 429 Too Many Requests → should say: rate limited, retry at [time from x-ratelimit-reset header]
  - 5xx Server Error → should say: upstream Pexels service is temporarily unavailable
  - 404 Not Found → the only case that should say 'not found'
Additionally, parseRateLimit() in errors.ts is dead code — defined but never called.

Fix:
  - Throw a typed PexelsApiError class that preserves status, statusText, and response headers
  - In a single shared mapping function, convert status codes to human-readable, agent-actionable tool errors
  - Wire parseRateLimit() into the 429 path to include a retry-after time
  - Add tests for each failure case: 401, 429 (with and without reset header), 500, and true 404

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #2 (High).

name: color param validation too restrictive
description: photoSearchSchema validates color with regex /^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/ (hex only). The Pexels API also accepts named colors (e.g. 'red', 'blue', 'orange'). Fix: expand the schema to accept either a 6-digit hex string (#RRGGBB) or a whitelisted set of named colors per the Pexels API docs.

name: README and server instructions describe invalid MCP behavior as intentional
description:
The protocol compliance bug (invalid image content blocks) is not just in the code — it is documented in two places as if it were a deliberate, correct engineering decision. This compounds the problem: maintainers may believe the behavior is spec-compliant, and future contributors will preserve the bug because the docs appear authoritative.

Locations:
  1. src/index.ts server instructions string:
     '...an image block with a url field pointing to the medium-resolution thumbnail...'
     This instructs the consuming LLM to expect an invalid content shape.

  2. README.md 'Engineering Decisions' table:
     'Dual content blocks — MCP spec allows text + image blocks per result. Image block may
      not render in all clients — markdown link in text block is the fallback.'
     This presents the broken behavior as an intentional design decision.

  3. CLAUDE.md 'Image content rationale' section:
     Describes using url field on image block as intentional hybrid approach.

Fix (do after fixing the protocol compliance bug itself, task 14uufydg):
  - Rewrite server instructions to describe what valid content shapes are actually returned
  - Update README Engineering Decisions to reflect the corrected design (resource_link or text-only)
  - Remove or correct the CLAUDE.md image content rationale section
  - Add a Compatibility section to README documenting what MCP client was tested against
  - Replace Claude Desktop config snippet with an official SDK smoke-test example showing tools/list + tools/call working end-to-end

Cross-reference: Pi evaluation finding #6 (Medium). Depends on: 14uufydg (protocol compliance fix).

name: Unconventional import placement in tool files
description: In photo-search.ts, video-search.ts, and get-details.ts the 'import type { McpServer }' statement appears at the bottom of the file, after the handler functions. All imports should be at the top of the file per TypeScript convention. Not a runtime bug but makes the files harder to scan.
result: Resolved as side-effect of 14uufydg. All tool files were rewritten with imports at the top.
